hypothesis: If universal machine-learning interatomic potentials trained on diverse datasets are applied to metal–organic frameworks, then they will reproduce DFT-reference structural optimizations, molecular-dynamics stability, bulk moduli, and heat capacities with DFT-comparable accuracy across a broad set of MOF and nanoporous structures because modern equivariant GNN and transformer architectures capture the essential interatomic physics (including dispersion) needed for accurate property prediction.
